config.detail: Top-level model_type=lasr_ctc with nested encoder_config (model_type=lasr_encoder). encoder hidden_size=512, num_hidden_layers=17, num_attention_heads=8 (num_key_value_heads=8 = MHA, not GQA), intermediate_size=2048, hidden_act=silu, conv_kernel_size=32, batch_norm_momentum=0.01, layerdrop=0.1, dropout=0.1, layer_norm_eps=1e-6. Subsampling: single stride-2 conv (subsampling_conv_kernel_size=5, subsampling_conv_channels=256) — 2x downsampling only (not 4x/8x like FastConformer). Positional: RoPE (rope_theta=10000.0, rope_type=default, max_position_embeddings=10000) — NOT relative-position attention. Macaron-style FFN scaling: feed_forward_residual_weights=[1.5, 0.5]; conformer conv module scaling: conv_residual_weights=[2.0, 1.0]. CTC head: vocab_size=512, pad_token_id=0 (CTC blank `<epsilon>`).

preprocessor.detail: LASRFeatureExtractor / LASRProcessor. sampling_rate=16000, feature_size=128 (n_mels), n_fft=512, hop_length=160, win_length=400. The preprocessor config does NOT declare window type, preemphasis, dither, padding_mode, mel_filterbank_norm, or log compression scheme — these defaults live inside the LASRFeatureExtractor class in transformers (v5.0 dev). Stage 2 oracle dump must pin them by reading the feature_extractor source and/or instrumenting a forward pass.

tokenizer_config.detail: tokenizer_class=LasrTokenizer (HF fast tokenizers backend). pad_token=`<epsilon>` (id 0 = CTC blank), eos_token=`</s>`, unk_token=`<unk>` (unk_id=3). 100 `<extra_id_N>` sentinel tokens declared (T5-style) at ids 513-612, lying ABOVE the model's effective output vocab (vocab_size=512) — they exist in the tokenizer but are never emitted by the CTC head.

tokenizer_json.detail: Fast-tokenizer JSON. Layout: id 0=`<epsilon>` (blank), id 1=`<s>`, id 2=`</s>`, id 3=`<unk>`, ids 4-511=SentencePiece pieces, id 512=`<pad>` (out of model output dim), ids 513-612=`<extra_id_0..99>` (T5 sentinels, also out of model output dim). vocab_sha256 captured by mechanical inspect.

spiece_model.detail: SentencePiece protobuf (~241 KB). The tokenizer.json is the HF fast wrapper around this SP model plus the special-token additions.

generation_config.detail: Not applicable to a pure CTC head — there is no autoregressive generation. CTC decode is greedy collapse-repeats + drop-blanks (or 6-gram KenLM beam, Stage 5+).

safetensors_metadata.detail: F32=351, I64=17. 105.28M params F32 — agrees with model-card claim of 105M. Single safetensors shard (model.safetensors).

language_model.detail: 6-gram KenLM (~700 MB binary, ~240 MB arpa.xz). Used by the publisher's headline WER table (e.g. RAD-DICT 6.6%->4.6% with LM). OUT OF SCOPE for the first ship — pure greedy CTC decode only. Adding LM beam decoding is a follow-up port concern, not a Stage 4 obligation.

model_card.detail: Conformer + CTC, 105M params, English-only medical-dictation ASR. Pre-trained on LibriHeavy 50k h, fine-tuned on ~5k h proprietary de-identified physician dictations (radiology, internal medicine, family medicine). Publisher headline WER is on RAD-DICT / GENERAL-DICT / FM-DICT / Eye Gaze — NOT LibriSpeech. Reference inference path is HF transformers (`AutoModelForCTC` + `AutoProcessor`) on transformers >=5.0.0; README pins the dev commit 65dc261512cbdb1ee72b88ae5b222f2605aad8e5.

reference_modeling_code.detail: First-party HF transformers integration shipped together with the model release (transformers_version=`5.0.0.dev0` in config.json). Stage 2 instrumented reference will be a thin hook around the AutoModelForCTC forward + LASRFeatureExtractor.__call__ at the README-pinned commit. No NeMo / ESPnet / FunASR equivalent.

dtype.evidence: safetensors header F32=351, I64=17 — all floating tensors F32. config.json has no torch_dtype field (transformers 5.0 dev), so the safetensors header is the only declared dtype source and is authoritative for storage.

upstream_benchmarks.notes: Greedy CTC decode. With 6-gram KenLM: 4.6%. Internal Google dataset; not publicly reproducible.
Greedy CTC decode. With 6-gram KenLM: 6.9%. Internal Google dataset; not publicly reproducible.
Greedy CTC decode. With 6-gram KenLM: 5.8%. Internal Google dataset; not publicly reproducible.
Greedy CTC decode. With 6-gram KenLM: 5.2%. Publicly available subset of MIMIC; potentially reproducible but not the default acceptance dataset.

NOT REPORTED by publisher (medical-domain focus). Stage 7 still uses LibriSpeech test-clean as the acceptance gate; the Oracle reference baseline measured in Stage 2 establishes the number Stage 4/7 gate against, not a publisher target. Absolute WER is expected to be substantially worse than general-purpose ASR (Whisper v3 large=12.5% on Eye Gaze, MedASR=6.6% — domain mismatch goes the other way on LibriSpeech).

reference_rationale: First-party HF Transformers integration. Google ships `lasr_ctc` / `lasr_encoder` / `LASRFeatureExtractor` / `LasrTokenizer` model classes directly in transformers (version stamp `5.0.0.dev0` in config.json). The README pins a specific dev commit (65dc261512cbdb1ee72b88ae5b222f2605aad8e5) — the reference env must install from that exact commit because v5.0.0 is unreleased. No NeMo / ESPnet / FunASR / author-side standalone repo exists for this checkpoint.
known_risks: Bleeding-edge transformers (v5.0.0.dev0). Reference env must pin `transformers @ git+https://github.com/huggingface/transformers.git@65dc261512cbdb1ee72b88ae5b222f2605aad8e5` exactly. Any later commit may move LASR class internals; any earlier commit will not register `lasr_ctc`. transformers 5.0 also has API churn vs 4.x — env build will be more fragile than usual.

LASRFeatureExtractor defaults not declared in preprocessor_config.json. Window type, preemphasis, dither, mel_filterbank_norm (slaney vs htk), padding_mode, log compression (log vs log10 vs log1p), and STFT center=True/False all need to be pinned from the feature_extraction_lasr source at the README-pinned commit during Stage 2 oracle dump. This is the single largest frontend ambiguity for the port.

RoPE positional encoding inside the Conformer encoder. Most ASR Conformer ports in this repo (parakeet, sensevoice, funasr_nano) use relative-position or sinusoidal attention. RoPE in an ASR encoder is unusual; Stage 4 needs a RoPE-aware MHA path matching the existing audio-llm/qwen3_asr LLM rope_theta=10000 default but applied per-encoder-layer.

Macaron-FFN residual scaling: feed_forward_residual_weights=[1.5, 0.5]. Existing parakeet/sensevoice Conformer FFN residuals are unscaled (or use the standard 0.5 macaron half-step). The 1.5/0.5 pair is non-standard and must be hard-coded into the converter+C++; getting either scalar wrong is a silent accuracy regression that won't show up as a shape mismatch.
Conformer conv-module residual scaling: conv_residual_weights=[2.0, 1.0]. Same risk as above — non-standard scalars applied around the depthwise-conv module.

Subsampling is TWO stride-2 conv layers stacked (Conv1d->Conv1d, both kernel=5/stride=2) — verified by reading LasrEncoderSubsampling.forward and PreTrainedModel._get_subsampling_output_length (num_layers=2). Effective downsampling = 4x. Output frame stride = 40 ms (25 fps) — same as parakeet/whisper, NOT the 8x fastconformer pattern. (Note: config.json only lists one (channels, kernel, stride) triple; both convs reuse the same params. Single-conv reading of the config would be wrong.)

CTC blank id = 0 (`<epsilon>`), NOT the last id. Existing parakeet/sensevoice CTC convention is blank=last-id; medasr inverts this. The converter must emit the correct blank id KV and the CTC decoder must not assume `blank == vocab_size - 1`.

Tokenizer vocab (613 entries: 512 SP + 1 pad + 100 extra_id sentinels) is wider than the model output dim (512). Ids 512-612 exist in the tokenizer but the CTC head's softmax is only 512-wide, so they can never be emitted. Converter should ship the 512-entry vocab used by the model head, not the full 613-entry tokenizer vocab.

Publisher does NOT report LibriSpeech test-clean. All publisher numbers are on internal medical dictation sets (RAD-DICT/GENERAL-DICT/FM-DICT) or on MIMIC Eye Gaze. The Stage 7 acceptance gate falls back to the measured Oracle reference baseline on LibriSpeech test-clean (per the `project_upstream_wer_unreproducible` memory). Absolute WER on test-clean is expected to be worse than general ASR models because of the medical-domain training distribution; that gap is not a port defect.
6-gram KenLM (~700 MB) shipped alongside the model is OUT OF SCOPE for the first ship. Pure greedy CTC decode only. Adding KenLM beam decoding is a future capability and is not a Stage 4 obligation.

Long-form inference uses HF pipeline `chunk_length_s=20, stride_length_s=2` (chunked offline batching, not real-time streaming). The model is not streaming-capable in the cache-aware/incremental sense — capabilities.streaming=false.

intake_gaps.reason: Not declared in preprocessor_config.json. Resolve from LASRFeatureExtractor source (transformers @ pinned commit) at Stage 2 oracle dump.
Not declared in preprocessor_config.json. Per-feature CMVN, per-utterance norm, or no normalization — resolve at Stage 2.
Not declared in preprocessor_config.json. Could be 0.97 (parakeet/whisper convention) or null. Resolve at Stage 2.
Not declared in preprocessor_config.json. Typical inference-time values: 0.0 or 1e-5. Resolve at Stage 2.
Not declared in preprocessor_config.json. STFT center=True/False affects frame count and t=0 padding semantics. Resolve at Stage 2.
Not declared in preprocessor_config.json. Typically `reflect` for torch / `zero` for kaldi. Resolve at Stage 2.
Not declared in preprocessor_config.json. Slaney vs HTK mel basis is a classic numerical-mismatch source. Resolve at Stage 2.

Declared as `sentencepiece` (spiece.model ships and the README cites it), but the runtime path is HF fast-tokenizer (`LasrTokenizer` backend=tokenizers). For Stage 4 the converter consumes the SP model; the type label tracks the underlying model, not the wrapper.
